preamble: The debuccalization of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the glottal fricative [h] is a documented form of lenition, typically occurring in colloquial speech where oral articulation is weakened and replaced by glottal friction.

examples.note: Casual speech variation where the voiced sibilant is realized as a soft glottal fricative.
While the shift is most prominent for voiceless [s], the voiced counterpart follows parallel paths of weakening in specific clusters or environments.

sources: Portuguese dialectology research on fricative weakening.
